summary: Completed backend-only regression testing for Android audio-control bridge fixes. Validated JUCE functional processor mode, raw-to-normalized parameter conversion, event/value routing in JS bridge, and transformed index/view script syntax.
action_items: Run a real Android device/emulator smoke test to verify audible behavior changes (Generate/Play/Stop/Waveform) under runtime audio thread conditions.
If controls still appear non-responsive at runtime, capture logcat around Param set/Event fired lines to confirm endpoint IDs and values reaching native layer.
critical_code_review_comments: No critical defects found in the referenced bridge/template files for requested routing and normalization fixes.
Current validation is static/template + syntax regression; runtime DSP behavior still needs device-level confirmation.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Added 4 focused regression tests covering: Engine::create functional JUCE processor path, sendParameter convertTo0to1 normalization before setValueNotifyingHost, index bridge routing split (eventEndpoints->sendEvent, others->sendParameter), and event endpoint set parity with Main.cmajor event/value declarations.
rca of the issue: Original non-responsive controls were likely caused by a combined bridge mismatch: processor fallback mode disabled effective parameter handling, JS sent all controls as events, and native parameter path expected normalized values while UI sent raw values. The patched code now aligns all three pathways. No MOCKED APIs used.
